description: Phase 1 dual-backend orchestration: extend portfolio/milestone system with two interchangeable backends (local file store and Kanboard Plugin API). Adds SDK resource modules for the Portfolio plugin's 28 JSON-RPC endpoints, a backend abstraction layer via typing.Protocol, configuration-driven backend selection (TOML/env/CLI), plugin detection, bidirectional migration tooling, and comprehensive tests.

userStories.title: Plugin SDK models — PluginPortfolio, PluginMilestone, PluginMilestoneProgress
userStories.description: Add 3 new dataclasses to src/kanboard/models.py representing server-side plugin entities: PluginPortfolio (id, name, description, owner_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at), PluginMilestone (id, portfolio_id, name, description, target_date, status, color_id, owner_id, created_at, updated_at), and PluginMilestoneProgress (milestone_id, total, completed, percent, is_at_risk, is_overdue). Each must have a from_api(cls, data: dict) classmethod following the standard resource model pattern with _int(), _parse_date() type coercion. These are distinct from the Phase 0 orchestration models (Portfolio, Milestone, MilestoneProgress) which are client-side constructs without from_api().
acceptanceCriteria: PluginPortfolio dataclass with from_api() handling all fields including _int() for id/owner_id/is_active and _parse_date() for created_at/updated_at
PluginMilestone dataclass with from_api() handling all fields including _int() for id/portfolio_id/status/owner_id, _parse_date() for target_date/created_at/updated_at, and str default for color_id
PluginMilestoneProgress dataclass with from_api() handling _int() for milestone_id/total/completed and float for percent, bool for is_at_risk/is_overdue
All 3 models importable from kanboard (added to __init__.py __all__ in sorted order per RUF022)
Unit tests with sample API payloads covering from_api() happy path, missing/null fields with defaults, and type coercion edge cases
Existing model tests still pass without modification
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: These are named PluginPortfolio/PluginMilestone (not Portfolio/Milestone) to avoid collision with the existing Phase 0 orchestration models in models.py.

userStories.title: Portfolios resource module — SDK
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/resources/portfolios.py with PortfoliosResource covering 13 plugin API methods: createPortfolio, getPortfolio, getPortfolioByName, getAllPortfolios, updatePortfolio, removePortfolio, addProjectToPortfolio, removeProjectFromPortfolio, getPortfolioProjects, getProjectPortfolios, getPortfolioTasks, getPortfolioTaskCount, getPortfolioOverview. Wire into KanboardClient as client.portfolios. Follow the established resource pattern (TYPE_CHECKING guard, _client.call(), typed returns, standard exception handling).
acceptanceCriteria: PortfoliosResource class with __init__(self, client) pattern
create_portfolio(name, **kwargs) -> int; raises KanboardAPIError on False
get_portfolio(portfolio_id) -> PluginPortfolio; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_portfolio_by_name(name) -> PluginPortfolio; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None/False
get_all_portfolios() -> list[PluginPortfolio]; returns [] on False/None
update_portfolio(portfolio_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
remove_portfolio(portfolio_id) -> bool
add_project_to_portfolio(portfolio_id, project_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
remove_project_from_portfolio(portfolio_id, project_id) -> bool
get_portfolio_projects(portfolio_id) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_project_portfolios(project_id) -> list[PluginPortfolio]; returns [] on False/None
get_portfolio_tasks(portfolio_id, **kwargs) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_portfolio_task_count(portfolio_id, **kwargs) -> dict
get_portfolio_overview(portfolio_id) -> dict
Wired into KanboardClient as self.portfolios = PortfoliosResource(self)
PortfoliosResource importable from kanboard (added to __init__.py exports)
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
Unit tests for every method with pytest-httpx mocking (success, error, empty/not-found paths)
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: 13 methods total. The resource follows the exact same pattern as TasksResource, ProjectsResource, etc. Dependency query methods (5) are added in a separate story.

userStories.title: Milestones resource module — SDK
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/resources/milestones.py with MilestonesResource covering 10 plugin API methods: createMilestone, getMilestone, getPortfolioMilestones, updateMilestone, removeMilestone, addTaskToMilestone, removeTaskFromMilestone, getMilestoneTasks, getTaskMilestones, getMilestoneProgress. Wire into KanboardClient as client.milestones. Follow established resource pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: MilestonesResource class with __init__(self, client) pattern
create_milestone(portfolio_id, name, **kwargs) -> int; raises KanboardAPIError on False
get_milestone(milestone_id) -> PluginMilestone; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
get_portfolio_milestones(portfolio_id) -> list[PluginMilestone]; returns [] on False/None
update_milestone(milestone_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
remove_milestone(milestone_id) -> bool
add_task_to_milestone(milestone_id, task_id, **kwargs) -> bool; raises KanboardAPIError on False
remove_task_from_milestone(milestone_id, task_id) -> bool
get_milestone_tasks(milestone_id) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_task_milestones(task_id) -> list[PluginMilestone]; returns [] on False/None
get_milestone_progress(milestone_id) -> PluginMilestoneProgress; raises KanboardNotFoundError on None
Wired into KanboardClient as self.milestones = MilestonesResource(self)
MilestonesResource importable from kanboard (added to __init__.py exports)
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
Unit tests for every method with pytest-httpx mocking
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: 10 methods. getMilestoneProgress returns PluginMilestoneProgress (server-computed, not client-computed like the Phase 0 MilestoneProgress).

userStories.title: Dependency query methods on PortfoliosResource
userStories.description: Add 5 dependency query methods to the existing PortfoliosResource: get_portfolio_dependencies, get_blocked_tasks, get_blocking_tasks, get_portfolio_critical_path, get_portfolio_dependency_graph. These wrap the plugin's server-side SQL-based dependency analysis, replacing the client-side DependencyAnalyzer graph traversal when the remote backend is active.
acceptanceCriteria: get_portfolio_dependencies(portfolio_id, **kwargs) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None; supports cross_project_only kwarg
get_blocked_tasks(portfolio_id) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_blocking_tasks(portfolio_id) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_portfolio_critical_path(portfolio_id) -> list[dict]; returns [] on False/None
get_portfolio_dependency_graph(portfolio_id, **kwargs) -> dict; supports cross_project_only kwarg
All 5 methods have docstrings and full type hints
Unit tests for each method with pytest-httpx mocking
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: These are added to the existing PortfoliosResource file, not a separate module. Returns are list[dict]/dict (not typed models) because the plugin's dependency response shapes are complex and variable.

userStories.title: Configuration extension — portfolio_backend
userStories.description: Extend KanboardConfig with a new portfolio_backend field (str, default 'local') supporting values 'local' and 'remote'. Add layered resolution: TOML profile key portfolio_backend, environment variable KANBOARD_PORTFOLIO_BACKEND, and CLI parameter cli_portfolio_backend. Validate during resolve() that the value is one of the two allowed values.
acceptanceCriteria: KanboardConfig.portfolio_backend field added (str, default 'local')
TOML profile key portfolio_backend read from config file
Environment variable KANBOARD_PORTFOLIO_BACKEND supported
CLI parameter cli_portfolio_backend accepted by resolve()
Resolution order: CLI > env > TOML > default ('local')
KanboardConfigError raised when portfolio_backend is not 'local' or 'remote'
Existing config tests still pass without modification
New unit tests for all 3 config layers and validation
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Independent of backend implementation — can be done in parallel with US-001 through US-004.

userStories.title: Backend protocol and factory — orchestration/backend.py
userStories.description: Create src/kanboard/orchestration/backend.py with: (1) PortfolioBackend typing.Protocol class defining the 12-method interface (list_portfolios, get_portfolio, create_portfolio, update_portfolio, remove_portfolio, add_project, remove_project, add_milestone, update_milestone, remove_milestone, add_task_to_milestone, remove_task_from_milestone); (2) RemotePortfolioBackend class wrapping PortfoliosResource + MilestonesResource to satisfy the protocol, translating name-based lookups to ID-based API calls; (3) create_backend() factory function that returns LocalPortfolioStore or RemotePortfolioBackend based on backend_type string. Verify LocalPortfolioStore already satisfies the protocol without modification.
acceptanceCriteria: PortfolioBackend Protocol class with 12 method signatures matching LocalPortfolioStore's public API
RemotePortfolioBackend class implementing all 12 protocol methods using PortfoliosResource + MilestonesResource
RemotePortfolioBackend.get_portfolio(name) calls get_portfolio_by_name() internally and converts PluginPortfolio to Portfolio model
RemotePortfolioBackend handles name→ID translation for all methods requiring portfolio_id or milestone_id
create_backend('local') returns LocalPortfolioStore instance
create_backend('remote', client=...) returns RemotePortfolioBackend instance
create_backend('remote') without client raises KanboardConfigError
create_backend('invalid') raises KanboardConfigError
LocalPortfolioStore satisfies PortfolioBackend protocol (verified by type checker / runtime test)
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
Unit tests for protocol conformance, factory behavior, and RemotePortfolioBackend method delegation
>=95% coverage on backend.py
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: The RemotePortfolioBackend must translate between the Protocol's name-based API (matching LocalPortfolioStore) and the plugin's ID-based API. This is the key adapter layer. Export from orchestration/__init__.py.

userStories.title: CLI global option — --portfolio-backend
userStories.description: Add --portfolio-backend Click option to the root cli group in main.py (type=click.Choice(['local', 'remote']), default=None). Pass through to KanboardConfig.resolve() as cli_portfolio_backend parameter. Store resolved value in AppContext.
acceptanceCriteria: --portfolio-backend option added to root cli group in main.py
Value passed through to KanboardConfig.resolve() as cli_portfolio_backend
kanboard --portfolio-backend remote portfolio list routes to remote backend
kanboard --portfolio-backend local portfolio list routes to local backend
kanboard --help shows the --portfolio-backend option
CLI tests with CliRunner verify both values
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Wire backend into portfolio CLI commands
userStories.description: Refactor src/kanboard_cli/commands/portfolio.py to resolve the backend via create_backend() instead of directly instantiating LocalPortfolioStore. Add a _get_backend(ctx) helper that reads the portfolio_backend from AppContext config and returns the appropriate backend. All 12 existing subcommands (list, show, create, remove, add-project, remove-project, tasks, sync, dependencies, blocked, blocking, critical-path) must work transparently with either backend. For the remote backend, dependency subcommands delegate to PortfoliosResource server-side queries instead of the client-side DependencyAnalyzer. The sync command becomes a no-op for the remote backend (data is already server-side).
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 portfolio subcommands work with portfolio_backend='local' (no behavioral change from today)
All 12 portfolio subcommands work with portfolio_backend='remote' using the plugin API
portfolio sync prints informational 'no-op' message when backend is remote
portfolio dependencies/blocked/blocking/critical-path delegate to server-side queries when backend is remote
_get_backend() helper resolves backend from AppContext config
All existing portfolio CLI tests still pass without modification
New tests verify remote backend routing for at least 4 representative subcommands
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: This is the most impactful wiring change. Take care with the dependency subcommands — local backend uses DependencyAnalyzer (client-side graph traversal), remote backend uses PortfoliosResource methods (server-side SQL).

userStories.title: Wire backend into milestone CLI commands
userStories.description: Refactor src/kanboard_cli/commands/milestone.py to resolve the backend via create_backend() instead of directly instantiating LocalPortfolioStore. All 7 existing subcommands (list, show, create, remove, add-task, remove-task, progress) must work transparently with either backend. For the remote backend, milestone progress delegates to the plugin's getMilestoneProgress instead of client-side computation.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 milestone subcommands work with portfolio_backend='local' (no behavioral change)
All 7 milestone subcommands work with portfolio_backend='remote' using the plugin API
milestone progress delegates to server-side getMilestoneProgress when backend is remote
All existing milestone CLI tests still pass without modification
New tests verify remote backend routing for at least 3 representative subcommands
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Simpler than portfolio wiring since there are fewer asymmetries — milestone CRUD maps cleanly to plugin API.

userStories.title: Plugin detection probe
userStories.description: Implement ADR-24: on first remote backend operation, call a plugin-specific API method and catch the JSON-RPC method-not-found error to detect whether the plugin is installed. Cache the detection result for the session (on the RemotePortfolioBackend instance). When the plugin is not detected, raise KanboardConfigError with a clear message including install instructions and a suggestion to use --portfolio-backend local.
acceptanceCriteria: RemotePortfolioBackend probes plugin availability on first API call
Detection result cached — subsequent calls do not re-probe
Plugin detected: operations proceed normally
Plugin not detected: KanboardConfigError raised with message mentioning kanboard-plugin-portfolio-management and --portfolio-backend local
Unit tests mock both detected and not-detected scenarios
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: The probe can call getAllPortfolios() and check for a JSON-RPC method-not-found error code. Cache the boolean on self._plugin_detected.

userStories.title: Migration subcommand — status and diff
userStories.description: Add kanboard portfolio migrate subcommand group with two read-only sub-operations: (1) migrate status — show current backend config, data counts on both local and remote sides, and plugin detection result; (2) migrate diff <portfolio_name> [--all] — compare local and remote state, show differences in portfolio/milestone/task membership. Both are non-destructive read operations.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard portfolio migrate status shows: active backend, config source, local store counts (portfolios/milestones/tasks), remote counts (if reachable), plugin detection status
kanboard portfolio migrate diff <name> compares local and remote portfolio by name, shows differences in projects, milestones, and task assignments
kanboard portfolio migrate diff --all compares all portfolios found on either side
Graceful handling when remote is unreachable (shows local data with warning)
Graceful handling when portfolio exists on only one side (shows it as 'local only' or 'remote only')
CLI tests with CliRunner for both subcommands
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: These are the read-only migration helpers. Split from the write operations for iteration sizing.

userStories.title: Migration subcommand — local-to-remote
userStories.description: Add kanboard portfolio migrate local-to-remote <portfolio_name> [--all] [--dry-run] [--on-conflict skip|overwrite|fail] [--yes]. Reads portfolio from LocalPortfolioStore, creates it on the server via PortfoliosResource + MilestonesResource. Handles conflict when a same-named portfolio already exists on the server. Supports --dry-run (preview without executing), --on-conflict (skip/overwrite/fail, default fail), --all (migrate all local portfolios), and --yes (skip confirmation for overwrite).
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard portfolio migrate local-to-remote <name> creates portfolio on server with projects, milestones, and task assignments
--dry-run prints all planned operations without executing any API calls
--on-conflict fail (default) raises error when same-named portfolio exists on server
--on-conflict skip skips the portfolio and continues (for --all)
--on-conflict overwrite removes existing server portfolio and recreates (requires --yes)
--all migrates all local portfolios
Progress reporting: prints step-by-step (creating portfolio, adding project, creating milestone, adding task)
Partial failure handling: logs error per portfolio and continues to next when --all
Summary at end: 'Migrated N portfolios (M failed)'
CLI tests covering dry-run, all three conflict modes, --all, partial failure
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Destructive overwrite requires --yes flag (consistent with project conventions). Migration is not transactional — partial failures leave partial state that can be cleaned up with remove commands.

userStories.title: Migration subcommand — remote-to-local
userStories.description: Add kanboard portfolio migrate remote-to-local <portfolio_name> [--all] [--dry-run] [--on-conflict skip|overwrite|fail] [--yes]. Fetches portfolio from plugin API, writes to LocalPortfolioStore. Same conflict resolution, dry-run, and --all semantics as local-to-remote.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard portfolio migrate remote-to-local <name> fetches portfolio from server and writes to local store with projects, milestones, and task assignments
--dry-run prints all planned operations without writing to local store
--on-conflict fail (default) raises error when same-named portfolio exists locally
--on-conflict skip skips and continues (for --all)
--on-conflict overwrite removes local portfolio and recreates (requires --yes)
--all migrates all remote portfolios
Progress reporting and partial failure handling consistent with local-to-remote
CLI tests covering dry-run, all three conflict modes, --all
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mirror of US-012 in the opposite direction. Can be parallelized with US-012.

userStories.title: Unit tests — backend abstraction and remote adapter
userStories.description: Comprehensive unit tests for orchestration/backend.py: PortfolioBackend protocol conformance for both LocalPortfolioStore and RemotePortfolioBackend, create_backend() factory behavior for all input combinations, RemotePortfolioBackend method delegation (each of the 12 methods), name-to-ID translation in RemotePortfolioBackend, and plugin detection caching. Target >=95% coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Protocol conformance tests: both LocalPortfolioStore and RemotePortfolioBackend satisfy PortfolioBackend
Factory tests: create_backend('local'), create_backend('remote', client=...), create_backend('remote') without client, create_backend('invalid')
RemotePortfolioBackend delegation tests: every protocol method verified to call the correct resource method
Name-to-ID translation tests: get_portfolio, add_project, add_milestone etc. resolve names to IDs
Plugin detection tests: detected, not-detected, caching
>=95% coverage on orchestration/backend.py
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mock PortfoliosResource and MilestonesResource at the method level (not httpx level) for RemotePortfolioBackend tests — the resource modules have their own httpx-level tests.

userStories.title: CLI tests — backend selection and migration
userStories.description: CLI output tests verifying: (1) --portfolio-backend flag routes correctly to both backends; (2) all portfolio and milestone subcommands work with remote backend (at least one test per subcommand group); (3) all 4 migrate sub-operations (status, diff, local-to-remote, remote-to-local) with CliRunner; (4) --dry-run, --on-conflict, --yes flag behavior on migration commands.
acceptanceCriteria: --portfolio-backend local and --portfolio-backend remote route to correct backend in portfolio and milestone commands
At least 4 portfolio subcommands tested with remote backend (list, create, tasks, dependencies)
At least 3 milestone subcommands tested with remote backend (list, create, progress)
migrate status tested with CliRunner (success, remote unreachable)
migrate diff tested (same, different, one-side-only)
migrate local-to-remote tested (success, dry-run, conflict modes)
migrate remote-to-local tested (success, dry-run, conflict modes)
All 4 output formats tested for at least one list command per backend
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mock both backends at the AppContext/create_backend level. Use CliRunner(mix_stderr=False) for clean output assertions.

userStories.title: Integration tests — plugin backend
userStories.description: Integration tests against Docker Kanboard with the portfolio plugin installed. Extend docker-compose.test.yml to install the plugin. Full CRUD lifecycle for portfolios and milestones via the remote backend. Migration round-trip test (local→remote→local verifying data integrity).
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.test.yml extended to install kanboard-plugin-portfolio-management into the Kanboard container
Integration tests for portfolio CRUD lifecycle: create, add projects, get, update, remove
Integration tests for milestone CRUD lifecycle: create, add tasks, get progress, remove
Integration tests for dependency query methods: get_portfolio_dependencies, get_blocked_tasks
Migration round-trip test: create local portfolio → migrate local-to-remote → migrate remote-to-local → verify local data matches original
All tests marked @pytest.mark.integration
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Plugin installation in Docker requires cloning the plugin repo into the Kanboard plugins directory and running the schema migration. May need a custom Dockerfile or docker-compose volume mount.

userStories.title: Documentation — dual-backend orchestration
userStories.description: Update documentation across 6 files: docs/configuration.md (portfolio_backend key, env var, CLI flag), docs/cli-reference.md (portfolio migrate subcommand group, backend selection notes on portfolio and milestone), docs/sdk-guide.md (PortfoliosResource and MilestonesResource sections, backend selection via SDK), CLAUDE.md (portfolio_backend config, plugin resource modules, PluginPortfolio/PluginMilestone models, orchestration/backend.py), AGENTS.md (new resource modules in directory structure), README.md (update orchestration section to mention dual-backend and plugin integration).
acceptanceCriteria: docs/configuration.md updated with portfolio_backend config key, KANBOARD_PORTFOLIO_BACKEND env var, --portfolio-backend CLI flag, and resolution order
docs/cli-reference.md updated with portfolio migrate subcommand group (status, diff, local-to-remote, remote-to-local) and backend selection notes
docs/sdk-guide.md updated with PortfoliosResource and MilestonesResource sections and backend selection example
CLAUDE.md updated with portfolio_backend config, plugin resource modules, new models, backend.py
AGENTS.md updated with new resource modules in directory structure

No placeholder content or TODO markers
